title: [BUG] getWorksheet(name) is case-sensitive while addWorksheet(name) enforces case-insensitive uniqueness
author: Grety
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

`Workbook.getWorksheet(name)` uses exact/case-sensitive matching, but `Workbook.addWorksheet(name)` rejects duplicates case-insensitively.

This can cause a false-negative existence check and then a runtime throw when adding a sheet.

## Lib version

4.4.0

## Steps To Reproduce
```Javascript
const wb = new (require('exceljs')).Workbook();
wb.addWorksheet('Sheet');
wb.addWorksheet('sheet'); // throws
```

### Actual behavior
`getWorksheet('sheet')` does not find existing sheet Sheet
`addWorksheet('sheet')` throws duplicate-sheet error

### Expected behavior
Either:

`getWorksheet(name)` should support case-insensitive lookup (Excel-compatible semantics), or
There should be an official API for case-insensitive lookup (e.g. `getWorksheetCaseInsensitive(name)`), with docs clarifying the difference.

### Why this matters
Many apps sanitize/normalize dynamic worksheet names before export.
Two distinct source titles can normalize to names that differ only by case.
Pre-checking with `getWorksheet()` can pass, and then `addWorksheet()` throws, causing export failures.

### Suggested fix
Keep current behavior for backward compatibility, but add a documented helper/API for case-insensitive lookup.
Alternatively, add an optional flag:
`getWorksheet(name, { caseInsensitive: true })`
